mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka clusters on Kubernetes. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kafka, making it easier to integrate with cloud-native applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Kafka Operator.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts/

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Strimzi Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the 'kafka' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for Kafka.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Strimzi Kafka Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Lists all pods in the kafka namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a Kafka Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster by applying a Custom Resource (CR) definition.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: Kafka
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: kafka
spec:
  kafka:
    version: "3.3.1"
    replicas: 3
    listeners:
      plain: {}
      tls: {}
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
      size: 1Gi
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
      size: 1Gi
EOF

```

> Creates a Kafka cluster named 'my-cluster'.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks if the Kafka cluster is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> Lists all Kafka clusters in the kafka namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Kafka cluster and any data stored in it.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the kafka namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your Kafka data. This step upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to a new version.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get pods -n kafka

```

> Backs up the current Kafka configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Kafka cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check your resource limits and ensure your configuration is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be caused by incorrect configuration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kafka' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Kafka cluster not responding
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Zookeeper service is down or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of Zookeeper pods with 'kubectl get pods -n kafka' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to Kafka broker
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to network policies or firewall rules blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your network policies allow traffic to the Kafka brokers.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.3.1, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka' and the status should be 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
